skill.name: tailwind-patterns
skill.description: Tailwind CSS v4 principles. CSS-first configuration, container queries, modern patterns, design token architecture.
skill.summary: Master Tailwind CSS v4 with modern patterns, CSS-first configuration, and container queries
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 77 potential issues (backtick execution patterns, weak cryptography, system reconnaissance) but ALL are FALSE POSITIVES. The flagged patterns are markdown code block delimiters (```), inline code formatting, and CSS framework documentation comparing v3 vs v4. No executable code exists - this is documentation-only content for Tailwind CSS v4 patterns.

content.user_title: Build Modern Tailwind v4 UIs with CSS-First Architecture
content.value_statement: Stop struggling with Tailwind configuration and responsive design. This skill provides CSS-first patterns, container query mastery, and modern design token architecture for building scalable UIs with Tailwind CSS v4.
actual_capabilities: Configure Tailwind v4 using CSS-based @theme directive instead of JavaScript config
Implement container queries for component-level responsive design independent of viewport
Create design token architecture with semantic color, spacing, and typography systems
Build modern layouts using flexbox patterns, CSS grid, and asymmetric Bento designs
Apply dark mode strategies using class, media, or selector-based approaches
Extract reusable components using @apply, design tokens, or framework components
limitations: Only provides guidance for Tailwind CSS v4 - not backwards compatible with v3 JavaScript config
Does not execute CSS code - generates patterns and configuration for developers to implement
Requires understanding of basic CSS concepts like flexbox, grid, and responsive design principles
Cannot validate generated CSS against specific Tailwind build configurations

use_cases.title: Migrate Tailwind v3 to v4
use_cases.description: Developers upgrading from Tailwind v3 to v4 who need to replace JavaScript config with CSS-first @theme directive

use_cases.title: Build responsive component libraries
use_cases.description: UI engineers creating reusable components that adapt to their container size rather than viewport breakpoints

use_cases.title: Implement design token systems
use_cases.description: Teams establishing semantic color, spacing, and typography scales using OKLCH and CSS variables

prompt_templates.title: Basic theme configuration
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Tailwind v4 @theme configuration with a primary brand color, spacing scale, and font stack for a [project type]
prompt_templates.scenario: Setting up a new Tailwind v4 project with custom design tokens

prompt_templates.title: Container query component
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a responsive [component type] using container queries that adapts from narrow to wide containers
prompt_templates.scenario: Building context-aware components independent of viewport

prompt_templates.title: Dark mode implementation
prompt_templates.prompt: Implement dark mode for a [page type] using [class/media/selector] strategy with proper color tokens
prompt_templates.scenario: Adding theme switching to Tailwind applications

prompt_templates.title: Modern layout pattern
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a [layout type] using [flexbox/grid] with responsive breakpoints and asymmetric Bento-style sections
prompt_templates.scenario: Building complex responsive layouts with modern CSS

output_examples.input: Create a dark mode card component with container queries
Set up design tokens with OKLCH colors

best_practices: Use CSS-first @theme directive in Tailwind v4 instead of tailwind.config.js for better performance and native CSS variable support
Prefer container queries (@container) for component-level responsive design over viewport breakpoints for reusable, context-independent components
Organize design tokens in three layers: primitive colors (raw values), semantic tokens (primary, surface), and component tokens (button-bg)
Use mobile-first approach by writing base styles without prefixes, then adding sm:, md:, lg: overrides for larger screens
Extract components when class combinations repeat 3+ times or when complex state variants are needed
anti_patterns: Avoid arbitrary values everywhere - use design system scale values for consistency instead of hardcoded numbers like w-[327px]
Do not use !important to override styles - fix specificity issues through proper component structure and CSS layering
Avoid inline style attributes - use Tailwind utility classes instead to maintain design system consistency
Do not mix v3 JavaScript config with v4 CSS-first patterns - migrate fully to @theme directive and CSS-based configuration
Avoid heavy use of @apply directive - prefer React/Vue components for dynamic behavior or pure CSS components for static patterns

faq.question: What is the main difference between Tailwind v3 and v4?
faq.answer: Tailwind v4 uses CSS-first configuration with the @theme directive instead of JavaScript-based tailwind.config.js. It also includes the Oxide engine written in Rust for 10x faster compilation and native support for modern CSS features like container queries and CSS nesting.

faq.question: When should I use container queries instead of viewport breakpoints?
faq.answer: Use container queries (@container) for component-level responsive design when building reusable components that should adapt to their parent container size. Use viewport breakpoints (md:, lg:) for page-level layouts that respond to screen size.

faq.question: What is OKLCH and why should I use it for colors?
faq.answer: OKLCH is a perceptually uniform color format that provides more consistent and predictable color adjustments than HSL or RGB. It is recommended for design tokens because it ensures uniform lightness and better color harmony across your design system.

faq.question: Do I need a tailwind.config.js file in Tailwind v4?
faq.answer: No, the tailwind.config.js file is optional in v4. You can configure all design tokens using the CSS-based @theme directive directly in your CSS files. The JavaScript config is only needed for advanced plugin configurations or legacy v3 migration.

faq.question: Should I use @apply or extract components?
faq.answer: Prefer extracting components using React/Vue/svelte for dynamic components with JavaScript behavior. Use @apply sparingly for static CSS-only components when you need to reuse class combinations without framework overhead.

faq.question: How do I implement dark mode in Tailwind v4?
faq.answer: Configure dark mode using the class strategy (manual toggle with .dark class), media strategy (follows system preference), or custom selector strategy in v4. Apply dark variants with dark: prefix like dark:bg-zinc-900 dark:text-white.
